review_title: Easy and Convenient Way To Store Passwords
description: While using LastPass, it was easy to store my passwords and sync them across different devices and web browsers. Additionally, the convenience was truly amazing because I could reopen my web browser and would not need to sign into LastPass again to autofill passwords and store them.
author: Jesse Z.
pros: It's an easy and efficient way to store passwords from multiple websites and across multiple web browsers. Additionally, I'm not asked to log in after reopening a web browser.
cons: LastPass makes it so that by default, you can only store your passwords in LastPass with it being difficult to store in Chrome's password manager as well. And, LastPass could've stored passkeys as well. Additionally, they make users pay to have LastPass across more than a certain number of devices.

review_title: Revolutionary password management: LastPass sets new standards.
description: I no longer have to remember passwords, neither my own nor those of my colleagues. This has made working much more time-efficient and secure. Plus, logging in once per day is extremely easy.
author: Spyros D.
pros: LastPass is revolutionary password management and authentication software. It impressively combines simplicity with the highest security. Key benefits are; intuitive operation and quick access to all accounts without entering a password, strong two factor authentication effectively protects all data, saves time every day through automated login processes and also fast and competent customer service. LastPass has exceeded my expectations and is an indispensable tool in my digital life.
cons: The browser plugins sometimes also covers fields that aren't login fields. But that's not really a problem, as it can be quickly clicked away.

description: I love that I can make folders in the app to organize my passwords better between personal and work use.
author: Stephanie P.
pros: I've used a few different password-management apps in the past, and I've always found LastPass to have the best UI and the most transparency around data management.
cons: I have the browser extension installed, and unfortunately it often populates in fields that aren't login fields. I've had to disable it on some sites due to that issue, but it's likely the same thing would occur with other similar apps.

review_title: Don't try to get in touch with customer support!
description: Trying to get a hold of customer support is a nightmare! They're holding my passwords hostage and there's no way to reach them. It's a horrible situation!
author: Jane R.
pros: The app is easy to use and works across all my devices. However, if you get locked out and need to contact customer support, you're out of luck!
cons: I've been locked out of my account for three days and can't get any help from customer support. I've tried everything: I opened a support ticket, sent four emails, and got no response. There's no phone number to call, and I can't use the chat feature because I'm logged out. I have my master password, but I can't access my account because I got a new phone and need to set up multi-factor authentication (MFA). The recovery options didn't work. It's especially frustrating since I'm a paying premium subscriber.

review_title: A Chrome password extension worthy of helping me save my passwords
description: If it wasnt for lastpass ive would have had to reset password on a lot of sensitive and critical websites I use on a daily basis. It has helped me secure all my streaming, bank info and website passwords without any sort of breach or data information being stolen.
author: Daniel J.
pros: The ability to save passwords, auto generate, secure your information and user friendly extension to autopopulate or update passwords is what made it stand out compared to others. They provide a master password you can generate to secure all passwords, and it considers personal questions for any sort of breach.
cons: Sometimes, between Google passwords and LastPass there would be an overlay on fields where I would need to add data from a dropdown, and with both working at the same time, i would have to deactivate it so it would allow me to see the options from the dropdown.

description: I like it. I decided to invest on it, since I was truly satisfied with the product. The free version is all you may need, but having online access makes it even better.
author: Ruben R.
pros: It is functional, agnostic of mobile platform and/or computer's operating system, easy to access, create folders and manage passwords.
cons: The online version does not allow to see the password with one click. It is not self explanatory that you have to click on the "wrench" the "Go" button gives you the impression that it will take you to the entry's settings.

review_title: Solid security for your information and credentials
author: Antti M.
pros: LastPass covers about all you need when you think of storing login credentials, credit card info, or other sensitive information. LastPass covers a wide variety of easy to use extensions to your pc/laptop browsers as well as the needed mobile apps. LastPass also offers solid and easy ways to enhance your security with external passkeys, such as physical Yubico's.
cons: There is not much I would say I disliked about LastPass. I ended eventually changing to another product which covers more than just storing credentials securely. Maybe that is the downside of LastPass.

description: The experience was awesome and very helpful.
author: Bahaa H.
pros: If you are looking for the best password manager you ought to try to read my review carefully. Lastpass is the best password manager worth all your time and money.Share passwords securely with colleagues with one click. Lastpass gives their users the ability to create strong, unique passwords for your account. Look no further, it's the best.
cons: I am not in a position to complain about it,it never failed us

review_title: Access and protect your digital and work-life anywhere and from every device with LastPass.
description: A five star experience you don't want to miss out. LastPass is the simplest,fastest way to share your passwords.
author: Pater N. K.
pros: With Last Pass, security is their key agenda. Safely store and autofill passwords. Reduce the risk of data breaches by using biometrics to login instead of your main password. It also gives it's users the ability to personalized their authentication and access controls of their organizations need.
cons: Nothing at all. It's worth all the time and money.

review_title: Does what it says on the tin: Password management with some useful features
description: Overall, makes me a lot more productive. I need a password manager, so it does the job but the added bonus of the autofilling on forms is great. I'm not sure what other features I'd like, perhaps easier backups both local and to a backup location of my choice E.g. my cloud storage would be good.
author: Sam W.
pros: The cloud integration/browser integration is the safest I've used. Really intuitive enabling you to autofill usernames/passwords saving a lot of time other alternatives where you have to search for the one you want
cons: Sometimes it's slow or laggy, especially if I have a load of tabs open, or am too fast/slow at clicking I can close it before it works. I do have the paid version but I don't feel like you get much more than the password management in terms of features.

description: It has been positive so far and encryption makes it safer.
author: Rakesh T.
pros: LastPass really helps in a team environment where an account for a tool is shared across multiple users. Given the nature of work I do it is very vital to have a password manager that works across different tools and accounts.
cons: Not very much worried of anything as it solves the problem I and the team have. Can't really comment on the admin side of things.

description: LastPass has made us more efficient with single-sign on. Our users are able to authenticate quickly and get into the application they are trying to logon to. The support staff has been very helpful and it was easy to get our account setup.
author: Ian B.
pros: We really like how LastPass securely stores our passwords so we do not have to remember each one for all the applications we use. The integrations with multi-factor authentication has helped us meet SOC compliance requirements. The way we are able to add and access our passwords has been extremely helpful.
cons: It is somewhat of a security nightmare if there was a hacker who got a hold of someone's LastPass account, as they would have quick access to their programs. The reliance on the user to keep it secure has been a concern for us.

description: Great experience. I've found their free plan is very complete and has many useful built-in features like dark web monitoring
author: Mariana V.
pros: Very useful friendly and has a free plan. All plans include dark web monitoring and password sharing which is pretty useful. Works accross multiple browsers
cons: They've had a couple of security breaches in the past. Which makes it hard to trust the platform completely.

review_title: Essential peace of mind for password protection
description: Essential peace of mind for storing all passwords and impotant technical information needed to navigate the online world.
author: Lisa M.
pros: I can't possibly remember all the passwords I need to use for different sites (not to mention 2FA authentification details, online codes, etc.) LastPass gives me peace of mind that I can put those details somewhere safe and yet accessible.
cons: It doesn't autosave, so if you close a window without saving you lose what you'd put into the entry.

description: Overall, LastPass is been a reliable tool for me and my team since the day we started using it. This makes my digital life effortless
author: John C.
pros: I love how I don't need to type or remember any of my password, safe in the vault and very easy to manage
cons: There was a moment where the extension lag a bit and the premium is kinda pricey

description: Overall experience with the last pass is good I would highly recommend it to anybody and everybody don’t knock it till you try it. You won’t regret it.
author: Alex A.
pros: Really great for software update updates super easy to use their customer support is top-notch Saves your login information Management is easy to use!
cons: Took a minute to get used to once you learn the program it’s not that bad just took a few times. Logging in to figure out.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Keep your passwords safe and individual
description: I have used Lastpass for more than a decade now in single user and group settings. It has helped me keep track of passwords as well as finally starting to generate individul passwords for accounts.
author: Hans Ulrich E.
pros: Reliable storage of passwords across devices. Wide range of templates for passwords, notes etc.
cons: 2FA login not very intuitive. Installation on Mac rather tedious.

description: LastPass is a great paid password management program to try out. It has one of the easiest interfaces to learn and use, so you can install it and use it without spending a lot of time on learning how its used.
author: Mike L.
pros: Easy to install software into your browser. Straight forward navigation of menus lends itself to quick and intuitive workflow.
cons: Its a subscription based service like many others in this space.

description: Most providing to the internet more dreams to come true anyway for sure.
author: Brett S.
pros: Most diically I'll nice modes no time to play around.
cons: No special enconvinence time to hustle a new late deal.

review_title: Was good but not enough with free version
description: Was a great experience but they have it gotten
author: Mitchell L.
pros: Been using forever but I have switched to something else
cons: Cost too much and only use on one device

description: It helped me stay organized and stop having to reset my passwords
author: Josephine K.
pros: It is a super great way to keep up with your passwords and usernames.
cons: Nothing, it is a wonderful addition to my home office computer

review_title: One of the best Password manager apps
author: Anastasios P.
pros: LastPass has all the functionality of a password manager.
cons: Nothing in particular. Maybe auto-filling passwords on mobile phones might require some time to properly setting it up

description: It's a solid app. I used it on MacOS, iOS, and iPadOS and had good experiences with it. Sometimes it can be tricky to find a password you want. It's in the vault and you have to find it, then click reveal. All in all, it does what it says and isn't terribly expensive.
author: Rex A.
pros: It's a solid password keeper, it has good iOS integration, and can suggest passwords.
cons: I didn't always find it as smooth to use as I'd like; sometimes it's cumbersome to get in and find a password.

author: Eddie W.
pros: It’s a cheap and straightforward system for my daily use.
cons: The family functionality is not that convenient and multiple users have to wait for authentication by main user

review_title: LastPass is a reliable and secure password manager
author: Aparna G.
pros: 1. Provides Highly secure password management with encryption and multilevel authentication. 2. Single sign on feature provides great user experience and ease of use. 3. Provides a central secure database for storing all passwords at one place
cons: I haven't faced any serious drawbacks with LastPass, overall greatly satisfied with the product
